task_completion.description: The draw.io application is open and contains a horizontal swimlane layout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io interface is visible with a pool/swimlane shape containing horizontal lanes.

task_completion.description: The swimlanes are correctly labeled with the five required branches.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The lane headers clearly read 'Main', 'Hotfix', 'Release', 'Develop', and 'Feature'.

task_completion.description: Commits are represented as nodes within the lanes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: There are at least 3 distinct commit nodes (e.g., circles or boxes) placed inside the swimlanes representing the chronological flow.

task_completion.description: Merge operations are represented by arrows connecting nodes across different lanes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Arrows cross between lanes (e.g., from Feature to Develop, Release to Main, Hotfix to Main) representing the 5 merge events.

task_completion.description: Tags 'v1.0' and 'v1.0.1' are visually indicated on the diagram.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text labels for 'v1.0' and 'v1.0.1' are attached to specific commits or merges in the diagram.

task_completion.description: The diagram is saved as 'gitflow.drawio' on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io window title shows 'gitflow.drawio' or a file manager window shows the file saved on the Desktop.

task_completion.description: The diagram is exported as 'gitflow.png' to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named 'gitflow.png' is visible on the Desktop, or an image viewer/export dialog shows the PNG file.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io GUI to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the draw.io interface with toolbars, menus, and the diagram being actively edited, rather than a script generating the file or downloading a pre-made image.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io export functionality to create the PNG.
integrity.visual_evidence: The draw.io 'Export as' dialog or file save dialog for PNG is visible in the screenshots.

integrity.description: The files are genuinely saved to the Desktop directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file manager or desktop view shows the newly created gitflow.drawio and gitflow.png files with appropriate icons, not just text files renamed to these extensions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The event log contains exactly 3 commits (Initial commit, Add login form, Bump version number to 1.0) and 5 merge operations (auth-module merge, release/v1.0 into Main, release/v1.0 into Develop, hotfix/v1.0.1 into Main, hotfix/v1.0.1 into Develop). The diagram must contain 5 horizontal swimlanes labeled: Main, Hotfix, Release, Develop, Feature. Tags v1.0 and v1.0.1 must be present. The final files must be saved at /home/ga/Desktop/gitflow.drawio and /home/ga/Desktop/gitflow.png.
